audit_scope: Recent NBA playoff LiveFlow and rolling-window tickets across NYK/PHI, SAS/MIN, DET/CLE, OKC/LAL

bankroll_state.reported_state: Bankroll at $0 after NYK/PHI Game 4 losses

audit_summary.primary_leak: Live team-total markets were attacked with insufficient garbage-time and bench-unit tail modeling.
audit_summary.secondary_leak: Bankroll and risk-control layer allowed aggressive exposure during an active drawdown.
audit_summary.core_diagnosis: The model has often been directionally correct on game script, but has failed to convert that read into the correct derivative market. Starter benching helped player-prop fades but did not guarantee team-total unders. Live entries also lacked required margin buffer and were too vulnerable to one-quarter scoring tails, hooks, and late bench production.

tickets.game: New York Knicks at Philadelphia 76ers Game 3
tickets.selection: YES - New York wins by over 5.5 points
tickets.root_cause: Strong read. Knicks control, Philadelphia late-game shotmaking limitations, and Brunson closer profile aligned.
tickets.patch_rule: Retain as positive example: live spread entries are preferred when game-control edge is clear and lead state supports margin continuation.

tickets.game: San Antonio Spurs at Minnesota Timberwolves Game 3
tickets.selection: NO - San Antonio wins by over 5.5 points
tickets.root_cause: The handicap correctly expected a competitive game but failed by a thin margin band. Spurs won by 7, clearing the 5.5 threshold despite Minnesota staying live most of the second half.
tickets.patch_rule: Do not attack NO/fade on a favorite margin unless projected favorite margin is at least 3.5 points below the market threshold. Reject if favorite has elite closer/paint anchor advantage.

tickets.game: San Antonio Spurs at Minnesota Timberwolves Game 3
tickets.root_cause: First-half tied game state created a false under look. Third-quarter pace and shotmaking reaccelerated, pushing total well past the live threshold.
tickets.patch_rule: For tied/one-possession halftime games, reject full-game under/NO unless both teams show sub-46% eFG and possession count is materially below baseline.

tickets.game: San Antonio Spurs at Minnesota Timberwolves Game 3
tickets.root_cause: Guard rebound live ladder was overvalued after halftime. Remaining rebound chances consolidated toward frontcourt and high-minute wings rather than Fox.
tickets.patch_rule: Reject guard rebound ladders after halftime unless player already has at least 75% of ladder target, minutes are secure, and team rebounding role is structurally elevated.

tickets.game: Detroit Pistons at Cleveland Cavaliers Game 3
tickets.root_cause: Cleveland had 64 at half but required 54 second-half points to clear. The model overweighted first-half scoring and underweighted second-half regression, Detroit defensive adjustment, and late clock compression.
tickets.patch_rule: If live TT over requires 53+ second-half points from a team already outperforming first-half efficiency, require a projected median at least 5.5 points above the line and no defensive regression signal.

tickets.game: Detroit Pistons at Cleveland Cavaliers Game 3
tickets.selection: NO - Cleveland wins by over 6.5 points
tickets.root_cause: Cleveland won by exactly 7. Model correctly saw Detroit competitiveness but attacked a thin margin band without enough buffer.
tickets.patch_rule: No margin-band fade inside 1.5 points of a common final margin zone. Require at least 3-point cushion from model median to line.

tickets.game: Oklahoma City Thunder at Los Angeles Lakers Game 3
tickets.selection: NO - Los Angeles Lakers over 107.5 points scored
tickets.root_cause: The model overweighted OKC defensive control and underweighted Lakers second-half offensive continuation/FT path. Live total state showed scoring pressure remained elevated.
tickets.patch_rule: Reject team-total NO/under when live full-game total is rising and target team has already crossed halftime pace expectation.

tickets.game: New York Knicks at Philadelphia 76ers Game 4
tickets.root_cause: Starter benching was correctly anticipated, but the model incorrectly treated that as team-total suppression. Philadelphia scored 31 in Q4 and cleared 108.5.
tickets.patch_rule: GARBAGE_TIME_BENCH_SCORING_TAIL_V2: if a team has 80+ through 3Q, reject NO/under unless line requires 32+ Q4 points or model median sits at least 6 points below the line.

tickets.game: New York Knicks at Philadelphia 76ers Game 4
tickets.root_cause: Blowout removed Embiid from Q4. The ticket required continuation minutes that the game state did not support.
tickets.patch_rule: Do not enter second-half rebound ladders for stars on teams trailing by 18+ unless player has already reached at least 70% of the target and coach incentive remains high.

tickets.game: Minnesota Timberwolves at San Antonio Spurs Game 2
tickets.root_cause: Minnesota suffered a 38-point loss. Blowout and Wembanyama paint deterrence suppressed Randle's interior activity and rebound path.
tickets.patch_rule: For rebound overs/ladders against Wembanyama, require stronger price or avoid if opponent blowout risk exceeds 18%.

tickets.game: Minnesota Timberwolves at San Antonio Spurs Game 2
tickets.selection: NO - San Antonio wins by over 10.5 points
tickets.root_cause: The model underestimated Spurs Game 2 response, Minnesota injury/minute fragility, and Wembanyama/Fox tone-setting after Game 1 loss.
tickets.patch_rule: REVENGE_BLOWOUT_RESPONSE_GATE: downgrade road underdog/favorite-margin fades after a superior home team loses Game 1 and has elite two-way anchor advantage.

tickets.game: Minnesota Timberwolves at San Antonio Spurs Game 2
tickets.root_cause: Spurs offense stayed efficient despite blowout. Model assumed pace/shot quality would decay earlier than it did.
tickets.patch_rule: No live full-game total under/NO when favorite already has 59+ at half and offensive leader minutes still project into early 3Q.

audit_findings.title: Team-total unders/no positions are the largest leak.
audit_findings.evidence: Losses on PHI no 108.5, LAL no 107.5, SAS/MIN no 206.5 total, and SAS/MIN Game 2 no over 206.5 all came from live scoring tails.

audit_findings.title: Starter benching logic is being applied to the wrong market.
audit_findings.evidence: Starter benching correctly hurt Embiid rebound outlook, but did not stop Philadelphia team total from clearing because bench scored 31 in Q4.

audit_findings.title: Margin-band fades are too thin for bankroll recovery mode.
audit_findings.evidence: SAS -5.5 fade and CLE -6.5 fade both lost by narrow margin bands despite competitive-game reads.

audit_findings.title: Rebound ladders need stricter game-state gates.
audit_findings.evidence: Randle 7+, Fox 4+, and Embiid 8+ all failed from role/minute/rebound-distribution issues.

audit_findings.title: Risk control failed after drawdown.
audit_findings.evidence: Aggressive LiveFlow continued while bankroll and confidence state were degraded.

patches_to_activate.rule: If bankroll is zero or active drawdown exceeds three straight settled losses, disable aggressive LiveFlow and allow only paper tracking until reset conditions are met.
If a team has 80+ points through 3Q, do not use starter benching as a standalone reason to bet team total under/no. Add bench scoring tail and require a six-point model buffer.
Reject team-total NO/under unless model median is at least 5.5 points below the live line or the required remaining scoring rate is materially unrealistic.
If a team has 60+ at halftime due to high shotmaking, reduce 2H projection by 4.5 to 7.0 points unless pace and free-throw environment both remain elevated.
No favorite-margin NO/fade unless model median favorite margin is at least 3.0 points below the market threshold.
Reject live rebound ladder if player requires Q4 minutes and game margin projects 18+ by early/mid 4Q.
Guard rebound ladders after halftime require player to already hold at least 75% of target plus a projected minute ceiling above 31.

new_liveflow_entry_requirements.correlated_exposure_limit: Maximum 1 live ticket per game until bankroll reset
preferred_markets_after_audit: Live spread only when control edge and market buffer are clear
Player prop fades after minutes kill switch confirms
Rebound unders/fades when blowout/minutes risk is active
restricted_markets_after_audit: Team total NO/under in blowout garbage-time windows
Full-game total NO/under after pace reacceleration

audit_verdict.rebuild_condition: Require at least 10 paper-tracked LiveFlow candidates with 60%+ graded process quality before real-money restart.
